response.body:
# SG-1500 — What you must collect


### Always mandatory (for every case)
- Beneficiary account number
- Originator account number


---


# If the party is a **Natural Person**


## Originator (Natural Person)
- **Name**
  - Name identifier type (enum)
  - Primary identifier
  - Secondary identifier
- ⚠️ **Pick ONE of the following:**
  - **Date & place of birth**
    - Date of birth
    - Place of birth
  - **Geographic address**
    - Address line
    - Address type
    - Building number
    - Country
    - Postcode
    - Street name
    - Town
  - **National identification**
    - Country of issue
    - National identifier
    - National identifier type
    - Registration authority


## Beneficiary (Natural Person)
- **Name**
  - Name identifier type (enum)
  - Primary identifier
  - Secondary identifier


---


# If the party is a **Legal Person** (entity/company)


## Originator (Legal Person)
- **Name**
  - Legal person name
  - Name identifier type (enum)
- ⚠️ **Pick ONE of the following:**
  - **Geographic address**
    - Address line
    - Address type
    - Building number
    - Country
    - Postcode
    - Street name
    - Town
  - **National identification**
    - National identifier
    - National identifier type
    - Registration authority


## Beneficiary (Legal Person)
- **Name**
  - Legal person name
  - Name identifier type (enum)

Go to the website above and create an endpoint.
Add that in the Notabene UI -> Setting -> Webhooks
Easily inspect, test, and debug incoming webhooks with the free Svix Play webhook tester & debugger.

request.description:
### Return a Transfer

This endpoint is used to return a transfer for a specific entity identified by `entityDID` and transaction ID `tx`. It allows the client to return the existing transaction details.

#### Request Format

- **Method**: POST
    
- **Endpoint**: `https://api.eu1.notabene.id/entity/:entityDID/tx/:tx/return`
    

#### Request Body

The request body should be formatted as JSON and might include the following properties:

- **reason**: (type: text) - why the transaction is overridden
    
- **return_settlement_id**: (type: text) - this must be in CAIP220 format
    

_Note: Each parameter must be provided as a separate key-value pair._

#### Response Structure

The response will return a confirmation of the transaction override, which typically includes:

- A status code indicating the success or failure of the request.
    
- A message detailing the outcome of the operation.
    
- Additional data related to the transaction if applicable.
    

Make sure to handle errors appropriately based on the response received.

### Override a Transfer

This endpoint is used to override a transfer for a specific entity identified by `entityDID` and transaction ID `tx`. It allows the client to override the existing transaction details.

#### Request Format

- **Method**: POST
    
- **Endpoint**: `https://api.eu1.notabene.id/entity/:entityDID/tx/:tx/override`
    

#### Request Body

The request body should be formatted as JSON and might include the following properties:

- **reason**: (type: text) - why the transaction is overriden
    

_Note: Each parameter must be provided as a separate key-value pair._

#### Response Structure

The response will return a confirmation of the transaction override, which typically includes:

- A status code indicating the success or failure of the request.
    
- A message detailing the outcome of the operation.
    
- Additional data related to the transaction if applicable.
    

Make sure to handle errors appropriately based on the response received.

### Freeze a Transfer

This endpoint is used to freeze a transfer for a specific entity identified by `entityDID` and transaction ID `tx`. It allows the client to freeze the existing transaction details.

#### Request Format

- **Method**: POST
    
- **Endpoint**: `https://api.eu1.notabene.id/entity/:entityDID/tx/:tx/freeze`
    

#### Request Body

The request body should be formatted as JSON and might include include the following properties:

- **reason**: (type: text) - why the transaction is overrriden
    

_Note: Each parameter must be provided as a separate key-value pair._

#### Response Structure

The response will return a confirmation of the transaction freeze, which typically includes:

- A status code indicating the success or failure of the request.
    
- A message detailing the outcome of the operation.
    
- Additional data related to the transaction if applicable.
    

Make sure to handle errors appropriately based on the response received.
